exercises.title: Simulate Image Metadata Operations
exercises.description: Simulate what Pillow's Image class does by building a MockImage class that tracks size, mode, and format. Practice the resize, crop, and convert operations conceptually.
exercises.starterCode:
class MockImage:
    """Simulates PIL.Image.Image for learning Pillow concepts."""

    def __init__(self, width, height, mode="RGB", filename="image.jpg"):
        self.width = width
        self.height = height
        self.mode = mode
        self.filename = filename
        self.format = filename.rsplit('.', 1)[-1].upper() if '.' in filename else 'UNKNOWN'
        self._ops = []  # Track operations applied

    @property
    def size(self):
        return (self.width, self.height)

    def thumbnail(self, max_size):
        """Resize keeping aspect ratio so neither dimension exceeds max_size."""
        # TODO: Compute new dimensions maintaining aspect ratio
        # If width > max_size[0] or height > max_size[1], scale down
        # Scale by the smaller ratio
        # Round to int, update self.width and self.height
        # Append ("thumbnail", new_size) to self._ops
        pass

    def convert(self, mode):
        """Return a new MockImage with the target mode."""
        # TODO: Create a new MockImage with same size but new mode
        # Append ("convert", mode) to the NEW image's _ops
        # Return the new image
        pass

    def crop(self, box):
        """Crop to box=(left, top, right, bottom). Return new MockImage."""
        # TODO: New width = right - left, new height = bottom - top
        # Return new MockImage with cropped dimensions
        pass

    def __str__(self):
        return f"MockImage({self.width}x{self.height}, mode={self.mode}, format={self.format})"


# Test
img = MockImage(1920, 1080, "RGB", "photo.jpg")
print(img)
print(f"Size: {img.size}")

# Thumbnail
img.thumbnail((800, 800))
print(f"After thumbnail: {img.size}")

# Convert to grayscale
gray = img.convert("L")
print(f"Grayscale: {gray.mode}")

# Crop
original = MockImage(800, 600, "RGB", "scene.png")
cropped = original.crop((100, 50, 700, 550))
print(f"Cropped: {cropped.size}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
MockImage(1920x1080, mode=RGB, format=JPG)
Size: (1920, 1080)
After thumbnail: (800, 450)
Grayscale: L
Cropped: (600, 500)
hints: if ratio < 1: self.width = int(self.width * ratio); self.height = int(self.height * ratio)
def convert(self, mode): new_img = MockImage(self.width, self.height, mode, self.filename); return new_img
def crop(self, box): l, t, r, b = box; return MockImage(r - l, b - t, self.mode, self.filename)

exercises.title: Image Processing Pipeline
exercises.description: Build a processing pipeline using the MockImage class. Implement a batch processor that applies a series of operations to multiple images and reports results.
exercises.starterCode:
class MockImage:
    def __init__(self, width, height, mode="RGB", filename="image.jpg"):
        self.width = width
        self.height = height
        self.mode = mode
        self.filename = filename

    @property
    def size(self):
        return (self.width, self.height)

    def thumbnail(self, max_size):
        ratio = min(max_size[0] / self.width, max_size[1] / self.height)
        if ratio < 1:
            self.width = int(self.width * ratio)
            self.height = int(self.height * ratio)
        return self

    def convert(self, mode):
        new_img = MockImage(self.width, self.height, mode, self.filename)
        return new_img


def process_images(images, operations):
    """
    Apply a list of operations to each image.
    operations is a list of (op_name, *args) tuples.
    Return list of (filename, final_size, mode) results.
    """
    results = []
    for img in images:
        # TODO: For each operation in operations:
        # If op == 'thumbnail': call img.thumbnail(args[0])
        # If op == 'convert': img = img.convert(args[0])
        # After all ops, append (img.filename, img.size, img.mode) to results
        pass
    return results


# Test the pipeline
images = [
    MockImage(3000, 2000, "RGB", "photo1.jpg"),
    MockImage(4096, 3072, "RGB", "photo2.jpg"),
    MockImage(1920, 1080, "RGB", "photo3.jpg"),
]

operations = [
    ("thumbnail", (1024, 1024)),
    ("convert", "L"),
]

results = process_images(images, operations)

print(f"Processed {len(results)} images:")
for filename, size, mode in results:
    print(f"  {filename}: {size[0]}x{size[1]} ({mode})")

exercises.title: Pixel-Level Operations with bytearray

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Aspect Ratio
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the thumbnail resize calculation.
warmup.starterCode:
width, height = 1920, 1080
max_w, max_h = 800, 800

ratio = min(max_w / width, max_h / height)
new_w = int(width * ratio)
new_h = int(height * ratio)
print(f"{new_w}x{new_h}")
print(f"ratio: {ratio:.4f}")
